name: Chicken with Cucumbers
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 pounds broiler-fryer chicken (2 1/2 to 3 pounds), quartered
2 cubes chicken-flavored bouillon or 2 teaspoons chicken flavored base stock
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook the chicken in hot salad oil until browned on all sides. Remove from the skillet.
2. Add mushrooms and garlic to the drippings in the skillet. Cook about 2 minutes.
3. Stir in flour until blended.
4. Gradually stir in sherry, bouillon or chicken flavored base stock, salt, and water. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is slightly thickened.
5. Add the chicken back to the skillet and heat to boiling.
6. Reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is fork tender.
7. Meanwhile, cut half of a cucumber into very thin slices and set aside for garnish.
8. Peel the remaining cucumbers and cut into large chunks.
9. Add the cucumber chunks to the skillet and continue cooking for 15-20 minutes or until both the chicken is fork tender and the cucumber is tender-crisp.
10. Stir in sour cream. Cook until heated through, but do not boil.
11. Arrange the chicken pieces on a warm platter, surrounded by chunks of cucumber and pour over the sauce. Garnish with thin cucumber slices.
